remember: Students will be able to recall the fundamental principles of evolution.
Students will be able to remember the key concepts of evolution by natural selection.
Students will be able to recall the role of mutations in evolution.
understand: Students will be able to understand the mechanisms of evolution, including genetic drift, gene flow, and speciation.
Students will be able to comprehend the differences between microevolution and macroevolution.
Students will be able to understand the concept of adaptation and its significance in evolution.
apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of evolution to explain the diversity of life on Earth.
Students will be able to use the concept of evolution to predict the outcomes of different scenarios, such as changes in population size or environmental conditions.
Students will be able to analyze the role of human activities in influencing the process of evolution.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the evidence for evolution from fossil records, comparative anatomy, and molecular biology.
Students will be able to evaluate the strengths and limitations of different methods used to study evolution.
Students will be able to synthesize the information from different areas of study to understand the complex processes involved in evolution.

ncert_objectives: Explain the evolution of man through the process of natural selection.
Describe the different stages of evolution of man.
Explain the concept of adaptation and its significance in evolution.
board_exam_objectives: To test the understanding of the fundamental principles of evolution and their application to explain the diversity of life on Earth.
To assess the ability to analyze the evidence for evolution from different areas of study.
To evaluate the understanding of the mechanisms of evolution, including genetic drift, gene flow, and speciation.
